scenario_results.reason: The lane resolved a standard talent travel and call-time question only when the call sheet, pickup plan, rooming state, and communication records aligned.
Missing production, talent, and schedule prerequisites produced a blocked-state response instead of speculative concierge guidance.
Unsupported requests to bypass studio teacher and permit controls were treated as authority-bound exceptions and routed rather than executed in-lane.
A talent-services conversation that turned into publicity and likeness handling was handed to the correct owner without implying rights authority.
Repeat pickup and rooming failures triggered an owner-routed escalation package instead of another generic talent update.
Per diem, billing, and loan-out payment questions were routed to business affairs or payroll instead of being over-resolved by talent services.
The lane rejected generic concierge framing and re-established production-specific evidence requirements.
Conflicting union and tenant policy handling triggered escalation rather than false-confidence synthesis.
Unsupported attempts to add uncleared talent to the call sheet were rejected cleanly and treated as readiness and authority issues.
Tenant-specific security, travel, and escort gates were applied as stricter overlays without losing the public labor and evidence baseline.

The in-scope talent-services scenario preserved production terminology such as call sheet, glam call, fitting, Exhibit G, final cast list, and studio teacher instead of collapsing into generic support wording.
Missing venue, permit, visa-owner, and contradictory arrival evidence triggered low-confidence escalation instead of confident synthesis.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and cross-industry contract.

The package stays inside talent services and does not claim casting authority, business-affairs approval, payroll disposition, legal interpretation, immigration determination, or child-performer compliance authority.
